name: Legacy Converter

converter_handles_all_legacy_formats.evidence: Tested v1.0, v2.0, and edge case manifests (list-based blocks_generated, missing manifest_version)

dry_run_mode_shows_diff.evidence: Dry-run mode displays legacy → v3 comparison with summary of changes

edge_cases_handled_gracefully.evidence: Handles missing participants (HITL flagging), missing dates (folder extraction), different status values, list vs dict blocks_generated

script_tested_on_5plus_legacy_manifests.evidence: Tested on 7 different legacy manifests across v1.0, v2.0, and edge cases

edge_case_handling: Missing participants → infer from folder name or mark for HITL
Missing dates → extract from folder name pattern
Different status mapping → v3 status normalization
blocks_generated as list → convert to v3 blocks structure
No manifest_version → detect by content keys
safety_features: v3 schema detection to avoid double conversion
Graceful error handling for malformed JSON

key_findings: Legacy manifests have 3 main variations: v1.0 (dict blocks), v2.0 (recall metadata), and unknown format (list blocks)
Participant identification is often missing or incomplete, requiring inference from folder names
Status values need mapping from legacy terms to v3 pipeline stages
Many legacy manifests lack meeting_type and require inference from folder patterns
blocks_generated field varies between dict (v1/v2) and list (edge cases) formats

features.dry_run: Shows conversion preview without modifying files
features.validation: Detects v3 manifests to prevent double conversion
features.error_handling: Graceful handling of JSON errors and missing files

recommendations: Test converter on full backlog before D5.2 migration execution
Consider adding --batch flag for processing multiple folders
Review HITL queue design to handle low-confidence conversions
